---
description: "Apply when reading any error-context.md file in test-results/. Reduces token cost by skipping sections with no diagnostic value."
applyTo: "test-results/**/*.md"
---

# Error Context Reading Rules

When opening an `error-context.md` file, skip the following sections entirely — they add no diagnostic value:

1. **`# Instructions` preamble** — always the same 4-line block; never read it
2. **`# Test source`** — duplicates the spec file; load the actual spec file directly if source context is needed
3. **`# Page snapshot` for API tests** — any file whose folder name contains `tasks-api`; status code + error message is sufficient

## Tiered Reading Strategy

| Scenario | Read | Skip |
|----------|------|------|
| API test failure | `# Test info`, `# Error details` | `# Instructions`, `# Test source`, `# Page snapshot` |
| UI TimeoutError (locator issue) | `# Test info`, `# Error details`, first 30 lines of `# Page snapshot` | `# Instructions`, `# Test source`, rest of snapshot |
| UI AssertionError (URL / value mismatch) | `# Test info`, `# Error details` | `# Instructions`, `# Test source`, `# Page snapshot` |

## When digest exists

If `test-results/failure-digest.json` was generated by the SessionStart hook:
- **Do not open any `error-context.md`** for initial triage — the digest contains all required fields
- Only open individual `error-context.md` files if snapshot inspection is needed (UI TimeoutError / locator failures)
